tldr: PortOne and Yuno are both multi-PSP orchestrators, so the move is mostly about replacing Korean-PG depth with broader LatAm/global routing — straightforward in shape, but you will lose PortOne's deep Korean ecosystem coverage.
why_switch: Yuno's orchestration coverage skews global/LatAm, while PortOne's strength is concentrated in Korean PGs
If your traffic is shifting away from Korea, PortOne's value drops
Yuno's documented lock-in is low thanks to its orchestration abstraction
Single API similar to PortOne, so the integration model is familiar
Consolidating on a single orchestrator reduces ops overhead if you currently run regional stacks
what_to_export: List of connected PGs and their credentials
Customer profiles and saved payment methods
Webhook subscriptions and event payload examples
export_method: Use PortOne's merchant dashboard and APIs to export transactions, customers, and configuration. Capture per-PG settings separately, since each underlying Korean/global PG may also hold authoritative data. Confirm exact endpoints, formats, and scopes in PortOne's official documentation before scripting bulk pulls.
import_method: Provision a Yuno workspace, connect the PSPs you intend to keep (note: not every Korean PG that PortOne supports will necessarily be available as a native Yuno connector — verify in Yuno's docs). Re-create customer objects, re-tokenize stored payment methods through the underlying PSPs where required, and rebuild checkout with Yuno's SDKs. Run the new flow against a small traffic slice first.

concept_mapping.note: Re-implement on Yuno's SDK; UI not portable
Different event schema; re-subscribe and re-map
Confirm field mapping in Yuno docs
Verify connector availability per Korean PG; some may need direct integration

gotchas: Confirm which Korean PGs (KakaoPay, Toss, NaverPay, KG Inicis, etc.) Yuno supports natively before committing
Stored payment tokens are usually PSP-scoped; switching orchestrators does not auto-port them
Localized Korean checkout UX from PortOne will need to be rebuilt — budget design time
PortOne docs are strongest in Korean; ensure your team has reference material for both sides
Webhook schemas differ — keep both old and new handlers running during cutover
Reconciliation outputs change shape; align with finance early

email.subject: Migration plan: PortOne to Yuno
email.body:
Team,

Proposing we move payments orchestration from PortOne to Yuno. Both are multi-PSP orchestrators, so the integration shape is similar, but PortOne's main strength (Korean PG depth) does not transfer one-to-one.

Plan:
1. Audit which Korean and global PGs we actually use on PortOne and confirm Yuno coverage.
2. Export transactions, customers, configuration, and webhook history from PortOne.
3. Stand up Yuno, connect required PSPs, rebuild checkout on Yuno SDKs.
4. Cut over a small traffic slice first; run both stacks in parallel.
5. Decommission PortOne after a clean reconciliation cycle.

Estimated effort: 2-6 weeks depending on PG coverage gaps. I'll verify connector specifics in Yuno's official docs before we commit.

Thanks
